description: This set of TEM bright-field micrographs show the γ-austenite microstructures of 20% cold-rolled Fe-20Mn-9Al-1.2C-xCr low-density steels, where subfigure (a) corresponds to 0Cr steel, (b) to 3Cr steel, and (c) to 6Cr steel. Each subfigure includes a selected area electron diffraction pattern taken from the [110] zone axis of γ-austenite at the top right, with a unified scale bar of 500 nm. {111} planar slip bands are observed in the austenite of all three cold-rolled samples. The average slip band spacing decreases significantly with increasing Cr content, measured as 371 ± 47 nm for 0Cr steel, 100 ± 15 nm for 3Cr steel, and 45 ± 13 nm for 6Cr steel. The figure confirms that higher Cr content refines slip bands after cold rolling, and smaller slip band spacing leads to stronger cold rolling reinforcement effect, supporting the conclusion that Cr addition improves the mechanical performance of cold-rolled low-density steels.
